ops.title: Operations overview
ops.subtitle: Live operational health: queue, media sync, transcoding, mirror and scheduler

failed.title: Failed jobs
failed.subtitle: Inspect, retry and clear failed queue jobs

confirm.retryAllText: All failed jobs will be re-queued.
confirm.deleteAllText: All failed jobs will be permanently removed.

scheduler.title: Scheduler
scheduler.subtitle: Manage scheduled-task visibility and activation — commands and timing cannot be edited
scheduler.notice: Task definitions (command and timing) are fixed in code and owned by the Laravel scheduler. Here you can only: enable/disable, add notes, and manually run whitelisted tasks.

modal.title: Manage: {{name}}
modal.desc: You can only control activation and notes
modal.enabledHint: When disabled, the scheduler will not run this task automatically

diagnostics.title: System diagnostics
diagnostics.subtitle: A safe operational runtime snapshot — no secrets. Complements the operations overview and health checks.

maintenance.down: The application is in maintenance mode
maintenance.hint: Maintenance mode is managed via CLI (php artisan down/up) — not toggled from the panel to avoid locking yourself out.
maintenance.debugWarn: Debug mode is enabled — it should be disabled in production.

cacheClear.title: Clear content cache
cacheClear.desc: Flushes the public content cache (articles, reels, sitemaps, categories); it rebuilds on first request. Does not touch system, session, or settings cache.
cacheClear.confirmText: Articles, reels, sitemaps, and categories caches will be flushed. This is safe but may briefly increase load while rebuilding.
cacheClear.auditNote: This action is recorded in the activity log (who and when).
cacheClear.noPermission: You don't have permission to clear the cache (cache.clear).
